steps.instruction: What makes a shape a quadrilateral?
steps.work: A quadrilateral is any shape with exactly 4 straight sides and 4 vertices.
steps.explanation: 'Quad' means four and 'lateral' means side. Any 4-sided closed shape is a quadrilateral.

steps.work: A rectangle has 4 straight sides and 4 vertices. Yes, it is a quadrilateral.
steps.explanation: We check the definition: 4 straight sides? Yes. So it qualifies.

steps.work: A triangle has 3 sides. It does NOT have 4 sides. No, it is not a quadrilateral.
steps.explanation: A triangle fails the definition because it has 3 sides, not 4.

steps.work: Square, rectangle, rhombus, parallelogram, trapezoid — all have 4 sides.
steps.explanation: These are all quadrilaterals because they all have exactly 4 straight sides, even though they look different from each other.

title: Classifying a Shape as a Quadrilateral

steps.instruction: A shape has 4 sides. Two sides are parallel and two are not. What type of quadrilateral is it?
steps.work: The shape has 4 sides, so it is a quadrilateral.
steps.explanation: First we confirm it is a quadrilateral by counting sides.

steps.instruction: How many pairs of parallel sides does it have?
steps.work: It has exactly 1 pair of parallel sides.
steps.explanation: Parallel sides go in the same direction and never meet.

steps.instruction: What quadrilateral has exactly one pair of parallel sides?
steps.work: A trapezoid has exactly one pair of parallel sides.
steps.explanation: This distinguishes a trapezoid from a parallelogram (which has 2 pairs of parallel sides).
